title: Oil extends losses as tensions ease in the Middle East
section_path: Oil extends losses as tensions ease in the Middle East
section_path_text: Oil extends losses as tensions ease in the Middle East
summary:
Main
Easing geopolitical risks continued to weigh on oil markets. Prospects of additional fiscal stimulus in China boosted sentiment across metals. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-10-30 113434.png | assets/2024-10-30_oil-extends-losses-as-tensions-ease-in-the-middle-east_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_99da29ce67d3.png

Market Commentary
Crude oil extended losses, as the fallout from the weekend's Israeli attack on Iran continued.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Easing geopolitical risks continued to weigh on oil markets. Prospects of additional fiscal stimulus in China boosted sentiment across metals.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Crude oil extended losses, as the fallout from the weekend's Israeli attack on Iran continued. Following the limited strike on military facilities within Iran, Israel's Prime Minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, said he plans to hold a meeting about a diplomatic solution to the war in Lebanon.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: China's imports of coal continue to rise despite stockpiles at record levels. This is being driven by ongoing fears of power shortages over the coming winter.
